title: Universal Proof Program
research_question: Is there a nontrivial universal structure underlying reasoning?
target_theorem: For every system S in the independently defined target class C*, and every admissible effective representation R in E*, if R satisfies the independently defined full reasoning-faithfulness contract P* for S, then the machinery closure M(R,S) contains an RCCD-equivalent kernel; RCCD is sufficient for P*, nontrivial, componentwise irreducible, and maximal relative to the frozen extension rules.

workstreams.purpose: Freeze the theorem, proof standard, outcomes, dependencies, and release gate.
Define the typed ontology and formal semantic objects.
Define C* independently of RCCD and prove neutrality obligations.
Formalize P* independently and classify all assumptions.
Define E* and its admissibility and effectiveness boundaries.
Define M(R,S) and prove hidden machinery cannot evade accounting.
Prove RCCD sufficiency by explicit reconstruction.
Prove component independence, irreducibility, and nontriviality.
Execute adversarial countermodel closure and prove the strongest supportable maximality result.
Assemble, mechanize, audit, and adjudicate the universal theorem.

release_gate.authorization_condition: Only a terminal UPP-W15 outcome may authorize public evaluation, and only if the exact theorem, assumptions, machine-checked proof status, unresolved obligations, and nonclaims are disclosed together.
blocked_before_authorization: claim that PR 280 is a universal proof
promote failed counterexample searches into deductive proof
